title: macOS no-terminal launcher: ship CubricVision.app (no Terminal) + start-with-terminal.command variant
description: macOS portable currently ships only start.command, which ALWAYS opens a Terminal window. Windows (start.vbs) and Linux (detached start.sh) both have a no-terminal default + a *-with-terminal variant; macOS does not. Fix: ship a minimal CubricVision.app bundle as the no-terminal default (double-click = no Terminal, also fixes the Dock icon via CFBundleIconFile) and rename the current start.command to start-with-terminal.command as the debug variant. The .app's Contents/MacOS/CubricVision launcher reuses start.command's body (quarantine strip + CUBRIC_* env exports + bundled Electron exec) but resolves the portable root 3 levels up from $0 (the .app sits at portable root). Build wiring: ditto on the CI mac runner already preserves the .app's symlinks + exec bits through zip/Safari-extract (no archive-writer change). Add appBundle + withTerminalStart to PLATFORM_CONFIG.darwin and dir-aware staging (copyDirEnsured + chmod inner script) in BOTH stagePortableSkeleton and the update-bundle launcher loop. Ships in 0.0.11 (new baseline). icon.icns available at build/icon.icns + media/icons/cubric-vision.icns. Verify on M4: double-click launches with NO Terminal; start-with-terminal.command still works; Dock shows the Cubric icon.
